_meta.description: Rules for extracting a complete component property schema from screenshots or Figma context BEFORE any Figma MCP build call. Used by Vision Agent (multi-input synthesis) and Spec Agent (property validation). Purpose: eliminate iterations caused by discovering properties mid-build.
_meta.mandatoryRule: For 2+ screenshots: property schema MUST be 100% complete and human-confirmed before the Orchestrator runs Step 7 (Figma MCP execution). Do not start building until every visual difference between screenshots has been assigned to a VARIANT value, BOOLEAN toggle, or TEXT property.

VARIANT.definition: A named axis where each value produces a structurally or visually distinct component version.
signals: Fill color changes completely between screenshots
Icon changes (different icon, not just visibility)
Structural layout changes (icon appears/disappears changes layout, not just visibility)

BOOLEAN.definition: A toggle that shows or hides a child layer. Does not change fills, layout direction, or text content.
BOOLEAN.figmaAPI: cs.addComponentProperty('Show Icon', 'BOOLEAN', true) — then bind child layer: layer.componentPropertyReferences = { visible: cs.componentPropertyDefinitions['Show Icon#id'].id }
signals: An element (icon, badge, tag, button) is present in some screenshots and absent in others
Hiding the element does not change the layout direction or parent frame size
The element is fully contained — removing it just collapses that slot

TEXT.definition: Exposes a text layer's content as an editable property in the Figma properties panel.
TEXT.figmaAPI: cs.addComponentProperty('Label', 'TEXT', 'Default Label') — then bind: textLayer.componentPropertyReferences = { characters: cs.componentPropertyDefinitions['Label#id'].id }
signals: Any visible text that would change per use (button labels, input placeholder, notification message)
Every distinct text string that a designer would want to edit without touching layers

classificationRules.rule1: When the same element is present in all screenshots but with different fills → VARIANT axis (not BOOLEAN)
classificationRules.rule2: When an element exists in some screenshots and not others → BOOLEAN toggle
classificationRules.rule3: When layout direction or structure changes between screenshots → VARIANT axis
classificationRules.rule4: When only content (text string) differs → TEXT property
classificationRules.rule5: When opacity drops below 50% AND colors are washed out AND no interaction → Disabled state (VARIANT)
classificationRules.rule6: When fill is darker than default AND no structural change → Hover state (VARIANT)
classificationRules.rule7: Minimum required TEXT properties for every component: at least one TEXT prop for each distinct user-facing label
classificationRules.ambiguity: When unsure if something is VARIANT vs BOOLEAN: if hiding it changes the overall frame size via auto-layout = BOOLEAN. If removing it would break the layout = VARIANT.

mandatoryOutputPerComponent.description: Vision Agent MUST output all three blocks for multi-input synthesis. Spec Agent MUST validate all three before building.
requiredBlocks: variantAxes — every axis with every value + evidence screenshot
mandatoryOutputPerComponent.completenessCheck: For every visual difference between any two screenshots, there MUST be an entry in variantAxes, booleanProperties, or textProperties. If a difference is unaccounted for, output it in unmatchedDifferences and ask the user before proceeding.

humanConfirmationRequired.trigger: After Vision Agent outputs the property schema and BEFORE Spec Agent finalises the spec
humanConfirmationRequired.rule: Do NOT advance to Spec Agent or Orchestrator until user replies 'confirmed'. This single confirmation prevents all property-discovery iterations.

multiQueryLibrarySearch.description: Rules for searching the Design System library for existing components. Always run all 3 strategies before declaring no match.

strategy1_publishedLibrary.matchRule: Any result with name similarity > 70% to the target component name = match

strategy2_localPageScan.method: Scan all pages of the current file via figma.root.children
strategy2_localPageScan.code: for (const page of figma.root.children) { await figma.setCurrentPageAsync(page); const found = page.findAll(n => (n.type === 'COMPONENT_SET' || n.type === 'COMPONENT') && n.name.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase())); if (found.length) return found; }
strategy2_localPageScan.fallback: Run when strategy 1 returns 0 results or confidence < 70%

strategy3_localDocsCheck.method: Check tokens/components/*.json files for a matching component name
strategy3_localDocsCheck.fallback: Run when strategies 1 and 2 both fail

confidenceThresholds.medium: Name similarity 70–99% → show match to user, ask to confirm
confidenceThresholds.low: Name similarity < 70% → no match, proceed to custom build

figmaLinkMeasurementRules.description: When input is a Figma link, ALL values must come from figma_get_design_context — never from visual estimation.
mandatory: paddingTop, paddingRight, paddingBottom, paddingLeft — exact px from context
fills[].color — exact RGB from context (convert to hex, look up in figma-variable-bindings.json)
fontName.family and fontSize — exact from context
width and height — exact from context
layoutMode — exact from context (HORIZONTAL / VERTICAL / NONE)
figmaLinkMeasurementRules.rule: Any value that can be read from figma_get_design_context MUST be used as-is. Visual estimation is only permitted for values absent from the context response.
figmaLinkMeasurementRules.confidenceLevel: All Figma-link-sourced values are high confidence. Never downgrade them.

tokenVariableNotification.description: Before and after Figma MCP execution, track variable state and notify user of any new tokens or variables created.
tokenVariableNotification.step_before: Before Step 7 (Figma MCP execution): snapshot all existing variable collection IDs via figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync(). Store as preBuildSnapshot.
tokenVariableNotification.step_after: After Step 11 (publish): call figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync() again. Diff against preBuildSnapshot.
tokenVariableNotification.output:
NEW TOKENS / VARIABLES ADDED
─────────────────────────────
Collection:  {collectionName}
New variables:
  • {variableName} — {resolvedValue} ({type})
  • ...

These have been added to your Figma file.
Update tokens/figma-variable-bindings.json if these are permanent.
tokenVariableNotification.rule: Always output this block even if the list is empty (output 'No new variables created').
